# Test MCP protocol validation and compliance

# Test protocol version negotiation
cat > protocol_test.json << EOF
{"jsonrpc": "2.0", "id": 1, "method": "initialize", "params": {"protocolVersion": "2024-11-05", "capabilities": {}}}
EOF

# Validate JSON structure with mcpspy
exec mcpspy --pretty < protocol_test.json
stdout '"jsonrpc": "2.0"'
stdout '"protocolVersion": "2024-11-05"'

# Test invalid JSON-RPC (missing version)
cat > invalid.json << EOF
{"id": 1, "method": "initialize"}
EOF

# This should still process but note the missing jsonrpc field
exec mcpspy --pretty < invalid.json
stdout '"method": "initialize"'

# Test method validation patterns
cat > methods_test.json << EOF
{"jsonrpc": "2.0", "id": 1, "method": "tools/list"}
{"jsonrpc": "2.0", "id": 2, "method": "tools/call", "params": {"name": "test"}}
{"jsonrpc": "2.0", "id": 3, "method": "resources/list"}
{"jsonrpc": "2.0", "method": "notifications/initialized"}
EOF

# Validate all method patterns
exec mcpspy --pretty < methods_test.json
stdout 'tools/list'
stdout 'tools/call'
stdout 'resources/list'
stdout 'notifications/initialized'

# Test with mcpdiff for comparison
cp methods_test.json methods_test2.json
exec mcpdiff methods_test.json methods_test2.json
stdout 'identical' 'files are the same'

# Cleanup
exec rm protocol_test.json invalid.json methods_test.json methods_test2.json
